evals.prompt: What am I working on today? I'm Malkio.
evals.expected_output: Agent searches Asana for Malkio's Addressing tasks (assignee=me or assignee=Malkio GID, Stage=Addressing, completed=false). Returns a prioritized list sorted by Q-rank/due date. If Addressing is empty, cascades to Focus. Does NOT return all tasks without a stage filter.

assertions.text: Agent uses asana_search_tasks with Stage=Addressing filter (not unbounded)
Results are sorted by due date or Q-rank, not random order
If Addressing is empty, agent checks Focus before reporting nothing to work on
Agent does NOT call asana_get_tasks or search without a stage filter

evals.prompt: Create a task: we need to redesign the onboarding email sequence. It's pretty urgent and Po should drive it.
evals.expected_output: Agent creates task with [BUILD] or similar prefix, sets Driver=Po (NOT assignee), Stage=Unsorted or Todo, Priority=Q1/Q2 based on urgency. Assignee is NOT set. Task goes to SteadyStars Development or D&S Daily Tasks. Asks about DoD/project if unclear.

assertions.text: Task is created with a clear, actionable name
Assignee is NOT set at creation (Driver field used instead)
Priority reflects the urgency signal from the prompt
Agent does not set followers speculatively
start_at/due_at or start_on/due_on is either set or agent asks about deadline

evals.prompt: Task 1213243591510417 is done — let's close it out.
evals.expected_output: Agent runs task-prep first (reads task + stories). Checks: all subtasks complete, DoD Status=Ready, artifact links present, no active blockers. If all good: posts summary comment, moves Stage to Resolved. If gates not met: surfaces what's missing specifically. Does NOT just mark completed=true without checking gates.

assertions.text: Agent calls asana_get_task before any update (task-prep)
Agent calls asana_get_stories_for_task to read comment history
Agent checks DoD Status before marking resolved
Agent checks subtask completion before marking resolved
A summary comment is posted before or alongside the stage update

evals.prompt: Show me what's blocking the team on SteadyStars Development right now.
evals.expected_output: Agent searches SteadyStars Development project (GID 1213243591510417) for Roadblocked tasks. Returns results with blocker context. Does not pull all project tasks. Applies project filter + Stage=Roadblocked filter. Identifies Driver/Owner for each blocked task as the contact.

assertions.text: Agent uses projects_any filter set to SteadyStars Development GID
Agent filters by Stage=Roadblocked (not all stages)
Response includes who to contact for each blocker (Driver/Owner/Assignee)
Agent does not load the full project task list without a stage filter

evals.prompt: I just realized we have no task for the API rate limiting issue we discussed. The context is in the SteadyStars NotebookLM. Can you set it up properly?
evals.expected_output: Agent: (1) checks if a related task already exists via typeahead, (2) references skills/context-validate to query NotebookLM for context on the issue, (3) creates task with [SOLVE] or [FIX] prefix, populates description from retrieved context, marks it for validation before writing. Does NOT create a vague task without attempting to gather context.

assertions.text: Agent searches for existing task before creating a new one
Agent references or uses context-validate skill for NotebookLM lookup
Task name has appropriate prefix ([SOLVE] or [FIX])
Assignee is not set (Driver used instead if context identifies owner)
Context from external sources is presented for validation, not silently written
